articles.title: صناع مسلسل رحلة طاهر المصرى يدرسون عرضه فى رمضان المقبل 2027
articles.summary: بدأت ملامح الخريطة النهائية لمسلسل «رحلة طاهر المصري» في التشكّل، بعدما انطلق تصوير العمل مؤخرًا، وسط اتجاه داخل الشركة المنتجة وصناع المسلسل لدراسة طرحه ضمن موسم دراما رمضان 2027، عقب خروجه من سباق رمضان الماضي لإتاحة الوقت الكافي لاستكمال التحضيرات والتنفيذ بالشكل الذي يليق بالعمل. ويواصل فريق العمل تصوير المشاهد الأولى وفق جدول زمني مكثف، …
articles.context: تأتي دراسة عرض مسلسل "رحلة طاهر المصري" في رمضان 2027 في ظل الظروف السياسية المتوترة في السودان، حيث يعاني البلد من صراعات داخلية منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. هذه الأوضاع أثرت على صناعة السينما والتلفزيون، مما يجعل عرض المسلسل في هذا التوقيت فرصة لتعزيز الثقافة والفن في فترة تحتاج فيها البلاد إلى رسائل إيجابية.

articles.title: Outdated and unfair: Why customary law can no longer deny married daughters their inheritance
articles.summary: For years, many Kenyan families have relied on traditional customs to deny married daughters...
articles.context: In Kenya, the 2010 Constitution and the 2013 Marriage Act aimed to promote gender equality and protect women's rights, yet customary laws still prevail in many communities, often sidelining married daughters from inheritance. This issue has gained urgency amid rising advocacy for women's rights, highlighted by the 2021 landmark ruling by the High Court that challenged discriminatory practices. As Kenya approaches the 2024 elections, the push for legal reforms to ensure equitable inheritance rights remains a contentious political topic.

articles.title: Why High court cleared way for police officers' murder trial
articles.summary: Were and Thuita had argued that Ipoa unlawfully influenced the DPP to prosecute them for murder.
articles.context: The case against police officers Were and Thuita arises amid heightened scrutiny of police conduct in Kenya, particularly following the 2017 election violence that led to numerous extrajudicial killings. The Independent Policing Oversight Authority (Ipoa) has been pivotal in advocating for accountability, especially after the 2018 agreement between the government and civil society to enhance police reforms. This trial reflects ongoing tensions between law enforcement and human rights advocates in a country grappling with systemic impunity.

articles.title: Why lobbies want Utumishi Girls barred from reopening Meline Waithera dormitory
articles.summary: Meline Waithera dormitory was partially destroyed by fire in which 16 students died.
articles.context: The Meline Waithera dormitory at Utumishi Girls High School in Kenya was partially destroyed by a fire on January 24, 2023, resulting in the tragic deaths of 16 students. This incident has sparked intense scrutiny and lobbying from various groups, including parents and education advocates, who are demanding a thorough investigation into the school's safety protocols and the circumstances surrounding the fire before any plans for reopening the dormitory are approved.

articles.title: Kenyan author who used AI loses copyright claim in landmark ruling
articles.summary: Here’s what the judgement reveals about AI-generated creations and their ownership.
articles.context: In 2023, the Kenyan creative landscape is increasingly influenced by technology, with a growing number of authors experimenting with AI tools for content creation. This ruling comes amid ongoing debates about intellectual property rights in the digital age, particularly following Kenya's 2021 National Intellectual Property Policy aimed at fostering innovation. The case highlights tensions between traditional copyright laws and emerging technologies, raising questions about ownership in a rapidly evolving creative environment.

articles.title: Property owner seeks to lift order blocking lawyer’s prosecution over Sh45m Nairobi villa sale
articles.summary: Monica Wambui has applied to join ongoing judicial review proceedings filed by advocate Chege...
articles.context: The case revolves around a disputed Sh45 million sale of a Nairobi villa, highlighting ongoing tensions in Kenya's real estate sector and legal accountability. Advocate Chege, implicated in the controversy, has faced scrutiny following a 2022 directive from the Law Society of Kenya aimed at enhancing transparency in property transactions. Monica Wambui's attempt to join the judicial review proceedings underscores the complexities of legal recourse in a market often marred by allegations of fraud and malpractice.

articles.title: The cost of mixing politics and business: Painful lessons
articles.summary: Wealthy business figures bankroll campaigns in anticipation of access to lucrative government...
articles.context: In Kenya, the 2022 general elections saw significant financial backing from wealthy business figures, notably during the campaigns of President William Ruto and his rival Raila Odinga. This trend has roots in the 2010 Constitution, which aimed to curb corruption and promote transparency but has struggled against entrenched patronage networks. The ongoing fallout from these elections highlights the risks of intertwining business interests with political power, particularly as the country grapples with economic challenges and governance issues.

articles.title: Kenya’s 15 billion-tree target falls behind schedule amid challenges
articles.summary: The country has planted 1.7 billion trees in four years, leaving it well behind schedule in its...
articles.context: Kenya's ambitious 15 billion-tree initiative, launched in 2018 under President Uhuru Kenyatta's administration, aims to combat deforestation and climate change. However, the program has faced setbacks due to funding issues, land disputes, and the impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic. As Kenya prepares for the upcoming 2022 elections, the government's environmental commitments are increasingly scrutinized, raising concerns about political will and the effectiveness of climate policies in the face of pressing economic challenges.

articles.title: Bitter campaigns for Ol Kalou parliamentary seat officially end today
articles.summary: They have been accusations of voter bribery and violence over the campaign period.
articles.context: The Ol Kalou parliamentary seat has been a focal point in Kenya's political landscape, especially following the contentious 2022 general elections that heightened ethnic tensions and allegations of electoral malpractice. The region has seen a surge in political violence, with local leaders and parties vying for control amid accusations of voter bribery. The recent campaigns have intensified rivalries, particularly between the Jubilee Party and the United Democratic Alliance, reflecting broader national political divisions.

articles.title: Thwake Dam to be completed in April, Ruto says as project stalls
articles.summary: "We had planned to complete it by December. However, it encountered problems after we ran out...
articles.context: The Thwake Dam project, a key component of Kenya's Vision 2030 development agenda, aims to provide water and hydroelectric power to the arid regions of Eastern Kenya. However, delays have raised concerns amid ongoing water scarcity issues exacerbated by climate change and political tensions surrounding resource allocation. The dam's completion is crucial for the region's economic development, especially following the 2018 agreement between Kenya and the African Development Bank to finance the project.

articles.title: Harambee Stars striker Ryan Ogam joins Belgian second-tier league side RFC Liege
articles.summary: The Belgian second-tier league, announced it had acquired the services of the Harambee Stars...
articles.context: Ryan Ogam's transfer to RFC Liege comes at a time when Kenyan football is navigating a challenging landscape marked by the Football Kenya Federation's ongoing governance issues and the suspension from international competitions by FIFA in 2022. This situation has prompted many talented players to seek opportunities abroad, highlighting the need for Kenyan football to revitalize its domestic leagues and restore its reputation on the international stage.

articles.title: Creditors seek to eject United Insurance liquidator Kamal Bhatt over Sh151m row
articles.summary: Creditors seek examination of Kamal Bhatt's conduct and the firm's finances nearly two years...
articles.context: The dispute over United Insurance's liquidation comes amid ongoing financial instability in Kenya's insurance sector, exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic. In 2021, the Insurance Regulatory Authority intervened to stabilize the industry, but allegations of mismanagement have persisted. The creditors' push to remove Kamal Bhatt as liquidator highlights concerns over transparency and accountability, particularly as the firm faces a Sh151 million deficit, raising questions about the handling of policyholder funds.

articles.title: Moment of truth for Uhuru and Ruto in Sh7 trillion public debt suit
articles.summary: State says hearing constitutional challenge before an appeal outcome would undermine...
articles.context: The Sh7 trillion public debt suit arises amid Kenya's ongoing economic challenges, exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic and rising inflation. Former President Uhuru Kenyatta and Deputy President William Ruto, who have had a contentious political relationship since the 2022 elections, now face scrutiny over their fiscal policies. The case's outcome could set a precedent for accountability in government borrowing, impacting Kenya's financial stability and governance moving forward.

articles.title: UN warns drone attacks deepen humanitarian crisis in Sudan’s Kordofan region
articles.summary: OCHA says strikes have caused civilian casualties and disrupted water supplies as cholera spreads across Kordofan
articles.context: The Kordofan region of Sudan has been a conflict zone since the outbreak of the Sudanese Civil War in 2011, with ongoing clashes between government forces and the Sudan People's Liberation Movement-North (SPLM-N). The 2019 ousting of President Omar al-Bashir has not resolved the violence, as power struggles continue. Recent drone strikes have exacerbated the humanitarian crisis, with the UN's OCHA reporting increased civilian casualties and a cholera outbreak amid disrupted water supplies.

articles.title: Sudan court sentences RSF leader to death in absentia
articles.summary: Ruling marks first court verdict against paramilitary leader since conflict erupted in April 2023
articles.context: The death sentence for Rapid Support Forces (RSF) leader Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo, known as Hemedti, comes amid the ongoing civil conflict that erupted in April 2023 following a power struggle between the RSF and the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF). This ruling marks a significant escalation in accountability efforts, as Hemedti has been accused of war crimes during the conflict, which has resulted in thousands of deaths and widespread displacement.

articles.title: Sovereign Wealth Fund is a good thing for the country
articles.summary: The Sovereign Wealth Fund should be anchored in socio-economic development.
articles.context: Kenya's establishment of a Sovereign Wealth Fund in 2023 follows years of economic challenges exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic and rising debt levels. The fund aims to stabilize the economy and promote sustainable development, particularly in light of the 2022 political transition after the contentious elections. With ongoing tensions over resource allocation and governance, the fund's success hinges on transparency and effective management to foster public trust and investment.

articles.title: Why ugali will be on the ballot
articles.summary: Depressed harvests and the resultant food shortage are never good news, but in an election...
articles.context: Kenya's 2022 elections were marked by intense competition, with food security emerging as a critical issue due to the severe drought affecting agricultural production. The ongoing conflict in neighboring Ethiopia has exacerbated regional instability, impacting trade routes and food supplies. As the country faces a projected 4.3 million people in need of food assistance, the political landscape is shifting, with candidates leveraging the pressing issue of food insecurity, particularly the staple ugali, to galvanize voter support.

articles.title: Kenya’s capital market has come of age; global investors are taking note
articles.summary: Kenya is no longer merely a frontier market with promise, but one delivering globally...
articles.context: Kenya's capital market has evolved significantly since the Nairobi Securities Exchange was established in 1954, with recent reforms aimed at enhancing transparency and attracting foreign investment. The government's 2021 Capital Markets Master Plan seeks to position Kenya as a regional financial hub, while ongoing infrastructure projects, such as the Lamu Port-South Sudan-Ethiopia Transport Corridor (LAPSSET), are expected to boost economic growth. This transformation comes amid a backdrop of political stability following the 2022 elections, fostering investor confidence.

articles.title: The great political masquerade in Kenya
articles.summary: If there is one skill many of our politicians have mastered, it is the art of adaptation.
articles.context: Kenya's political landscape has been shaped by the contentious 2022 presidential election, where William Ruto emerged victorious amid allegations of electoral malpractice. The fallout from the election has intensified political rivalries, particularly between Ruto's Kenya Kwanza Alliance and Raila Odinga's Azimio coalition. Additionally, the ongoing push for constitutional reforms and the fallout from the 2010 Constitution's devolution provisions have further complicated the political dynamics, making the current situation a critical juncture for governance and stability.

articles.title: Fix cricket from below
articles.summary: The basics should be taught at the primary school level.
articles.context: Cricket in Kenya has faced significant challenges since the national team's peak in the early 2000s, including a decline in grassroots development and funding. The 2003 Cricket World Cup, where Kenya reached the semifinals, sparked initial interest, but ongoing issues like the 2018 suspension of the Kenya Cricket Board by the International Cricket Council highlight the need for foundational reforms. As Kenya seeks to rebuild its cricketing stature, focusing on primary school education is crucial for sustainable growth.

articles.title: Expand SHA package for diabetics’ benefit
articles.summary: The Ministry of Health says nearly 800,000 Kenyans are living with diabetes.
articles.context: Kenya's diabetes prevalence has surged due to lifestyle changes and urbanization, with the Ministry of Health reporting nearly 800,000 cases as of 2023. The government has been implementing the Universal Health Coverage (UHC) initiative, which aims to expand access to essential health services, including diabetes care. However, ongoing challenges in healthcare funding and resource allocation hinder the effective rollout of the Social Health Insurance (SHA) package, making the need for expansion critical for diabetic patients.

articles.title: Sudan rights commission says 6,000 held in RSF-run prisons in El Fasher
articles.summary: Report accuses RSF of attacking civilian infrastructure, looting banks, destroying 8 airports, damaging 50 aircraft
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), has led to widespread violence and human rights abuses. The RSF, originally formed from militias involved in the Darfur conflict, has been accused of targeting civilians and critical infrastructure. The situation in El Fasher, a strategic city in Darfur, highlights the deteriorating humanitarian conditions and the urgent need for accountability amidst the chaos.

articles.title: 14 arrested over chaos at opposition events in Kisumu, Nyahururu
articles.summary: Goon attacks in two Linda Mwananchi events have deepened fears of escalating political violence.
articles.context: The arrests follow a series of violent incidents linked to the opposition party, Azimio la Umoja, led by Raila Odinga, amid heightened political tensions in Kenya. The events occurred shortly after the controversial August 2022 presidential elections, which saw Odinga contesting William Ruto's victory. The ongoing fallout from these elections has intensified fears of political violence, particularly as the opposition rallies support against the government's policies and seeks to mobilize grassroots activism.

articles.title: Kenya qualify for Fifa Under-17 Women’s World Cup
articles.summary: This is the second time Kenya is qualifying for the global football extravaganza.
articles.context: Kenya's qualification for the FIFA Under-17 Women’s World Cup comes amid a growing emphasis on women's sports in the region, following the 2019 launch of the Football Kenya Federation's Women’s Premier League. This achievement is particularly significant as it reflects Kenya's commitment to gender equality in sports, especially in light of ongoing challenges such as limited funding and societal barriers. The team's previous participation in the World Cup in 2016 marked a pivotal moment for women's football in Kenya.

articles.title: بنك السودان المركزي يتخذ إجراءات رقابية بحق عدد من المصارف لمخالفات تتعلق بالنقد الأجنبي
articles.summary: اتخذ بنك السودان المركزي عدداً من الإجراءات الرقابية والإدارية بحق بعض المصارف، وذلك استناداً إلى نتائج أعمال التفتيش والرقابة التي رصدت مخالفات لأحكام اللوائح والمنشورات المنظمة لعمليات النقد الأجنبي وذلك في إطار مسؤولياته القانونية الرامية إلى المحافظة على استقرار سوق النقد الأجنبي، والحد من الممارسات التي تؤثر سلباً على استقرار سعر الصرف، وتعزيز كفاءة التعاملات …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الإجراءات من بنك السودان المركزي في وقت حساس، حيث يعاني السودان من أزمة اقتصادية خانقة تتفاقم منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع. هذه الأوضاع أدت إلى تدهور سعر الصرف وارتفاع معدلات التضخم، مما جعل تدخل البنك المركزي ضرورياً للحفاظ على استقرار سوق النقد الأجنبي وتعزيز الثقة في النظام المصرفي.

articles.title: المفوضية القومية لحقوق الإنسان: الحرب أفرزت أكبر كارثة إنسانية في العالم
articles.summary: أكد الأستاذ كمال الدين الدندراوي رئيس لجنة إعداد تقرير مفوضية حقوق الانسان المقدم لآلية الاستعراض الدوري الشامل لمجلس حقوق الإنسان، أن الحرب السودانية أفرزت أسوأ كارثة إنسانية شهدها العالم. وأشار إلى أنها أدت لخروج 14 مليون شخص من منازلهم ما بين لاجئ ونازح، بالإضافة إلي نحو 25 مليون يعانون من نقص الغذاء. واضاف لدي استعراضه …
articles.context: تتزامن هذه الكارثة الإنسانية مع تصاعد النزاع المسلح في السودان منذ أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية. الاتفاقات السابقة مثل اتفاق جوبا للسلام في 2020 لم تُحقق الاستقرار المنشود، حيث أدت الانقسامات السياسية والنزاعات القبلية إلى نزوح جماعي وارتفاع معدلات الفقر والجوع في البلاد.

articles.title: وكيل الثروة الحيوانية يبحث مع وفد الرباطاب إنفاذ مشروعات إنتاجية بمحلية أبو حمد
articles.summary: بحث وكيل وزارة الثروة الحيوانية والسمكية، د. عمار الشيخ إدريس، اليوم بمباني الوزارة بالخرطوم، مع وفد عموم الرباطاب برئاسة ناظر عموم الرباطاب، خليفة الشيخ الصائم، عدداً من المشروعات التنموية والإنتاجية بمحلية أبو حمد بولاية نهر النيل . وتناول الاجتماع محاور متعددة شملت مشروعات تربية الدواجن، وإنتاج الألبان، والتحسين الوراثي للثروة الحيوانية، بجانب مشروعات صيد وتربية …
articles.context: تأتي هذه المناقشات في وقت حساس للسودان، حيث يواجه البلد تحديات اقتصادية كبيرة بعد النزاع المستمر منذ أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. تسعى الحكومة الانتقالية إلى تعزيز الأمن الغذائي والتنمية الاقتصادية من خلال مشروعات مثل تلك التي تم بحثها مع وفد الرباطاب، في محاولة لدعم المجتمعات المحلية وتحسين سبل العيش في ظل الظروف الصعبة.

articles.title: حكومة السودان تنعي صاحب السمو الأمير الوالد الشيخ حمد بن خليفة آل ثاني
articles.summary: أصدرت وزارة الخارجية والتعاون الدولي بيان تعزية اليوم نعت فيه المغفورله بإذن الله تعالى، صاحب السمو الأمير الوالد الشيخ حمد بن خليفة آل ثاني، الذي انتقل إلى جوار ربه بعد حياة حافلة بالعطاء والإنجاز، كرّس خلالها جهوده لخدمة دولة قطر وشعبها وتعزيز مسيرة نهضتها وتقدمها، وترسيخ مكانتها الإقليمية والدولية، ودعم قيم العدل والسلام والتعاون فى …
articles.context: تأتي نعي حكومة السودان للأمير الوالد الشيخ حمد بن خليفة آل ثاني في وقت حساس، حيث تشهد المنطقة توترات سياسية متزايدة، خاصة في السودان بعد اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. كما أن قطر تلعب دورًا مهمًا في الوساطة بين الأطراف المتنازعة في السودان، مما يجعل وفاة الشيخ حمد بن خليفة آل ثاني، الذي كان له دور بارز في تعزيز العلاقات الإقليمية، حدثًا ذا دلالة خاصة.

articles.title: Goons storm ACK Kisumu church hosting Linda Mwananchi leaders, one injured
articles.summary: Governor Orengo, Senator Sifuna and other leaders were attending a church service.
articles.context: The attack on the ACK Kisumu church occurs amid heightened political tensions in Kenya, particularly following the 2022 general elections, which saw Governor James Orengo and Senator Edwin Sifuna emerge as key figures in the opposition coalition, Azimio la Umoja. This incident reflects ongoing struggles over political power and community safety, exacerbated by recent protests against the government's economic policies and governance issues, particularly in the wake of rising living costs and corruption allegations.

articles.title: Goons and violence will not stop or intimidate us, Sifuna says after violence in Kisumu, Nyahururu
articles.context: The violence in Kisumu and Nyahururu follows heightened political tensions in Kenya, particularly after the August 2022 general elections, which saw William Ruto ascend to the presidency amid allegations of electoral misconduct. Raila Odinga's opposition coalition has been vocal against perceived injustices, and recent protests have escalated, leading to clashes with security forces. Sifuna's remarks reflect the ongoing struggle for political accountability and the resilience of opposition supporters in the face of intimidation.

articles.title: Somalia’s political crisis, deteriorating security and a “Questionable” agreement
articles.summary: At the beginning of this July, two documents revealed that the United States intends to prevent the United Nations from supporting the African Union peacekeeping mission in Somalia as of the beginning of next year, in a move that officials said would most likely bring the mission’s operations to an end, according to Reuters. The […]
articles.context: Somalia has faced ongoing instability since the collapse of its central government in 1991, leading to a protracted civil war and the rise of militant groups like Al-Shabaab. The African Union Mission in Somalia (AMISOM), established in 2007, has been crucial in combating these threats. However, the recent U.S. decision to restrict UN support for AMISOM, set to take effect in January 2024, raises concerns about the mission's sustainability and the potential resurgence of violence in the region.

articles.title: بألحانه وإحساسه.. رامى صبرى يقدم تجربة موسيقية متكاملة فى ألبوم “القمر”
articles.summary: نجح النجم رامي صبري في إثبات أن الاستمرارية في عالم الغناء لا تتحقق بالصدفة، وإنما بالاجتهاد والبحث الدائم عن الاختلاف، فمن خلال ألبومه الجديد “القمر”، يقدم تجربة موسيقية متكاملة تجمع بين التنوع في الأفكار، والصدق في المشاعر، وجودة التنفيذ، ليحصد الألبوم منذ طرحه تفاعلًا واسعًا من الجمهور عبر مختلف المنصات. الألبوم لم يعتمد على لون …
articles.context: يأتي إصدار ألبوم "القمر" لرامي صبري في وقت حساس بالنسبة للسودان، حيث تشهد البلاد توترات سياسية وأمنية منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. في ظل هذه الظروف، تلعب الموسيقى دورًا مهمًا في تعزيز الروح المعنوية وتوحيد المجتمع، مما يجعل تجربة صبري الموسيقية أكثر أهمية في تقديم رسالة أمل وتغيير في زمن الصراع.

articles.title: Ex-Jonglei minister Ajiba Ochang laid to rest in Juba
articles.summary: Isaac Ajiba Ochang, a former information minister for South Sudan’s Jonglei State, was buried on The post Ex-Jonglei minister Ajiba Ochang laid to rest in Juba appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: Isaac Ajiba Ochang served as the information minister for Jonglei State during a tumultuous period marked by ongoing conflict and political instability in South Sudan, particularly following the civil war that erupted in December 2013. His passing comes at a time when the country is grappling with the implementation of the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, which aims to foster peace and stability amid persistent ethnic tensions and power struggles.

articles.title: أحمد السقا يكشف وجهاً جديداً من موهبته في فيلم خلى بالك من نفسك
articles.summary: يستعد النجم أحمد السقا لمفاجأة جمهوره في موسم صيف 2026 من خلال فيلم “خلي بالك من نفسك”، حيث يكشف عن وجه جديد من موهبته الفنية عبر اداء يعتمد على خفة الظل والإيقاع الكوميدي، في ابتعاد واضح عن الأدوار التى سبق وقدمها طوال السنوات الماضية التى اعتمد فيها على أدوار الأكشن أو الأكشن الممزوج بالكوميديا. ويقدم …
articles.context: يأتي فيلم "خلي بالك من نفسك" في وقت يشهد فيه السودان تحديات سياسية واقتصادية كبيرة، خاصة بعد اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. في ظل هذه الظروف، يسعى الفنانون مثل أحمد السقا إلى تقديم محتوى يساهم في رفع الروح المعنوية للجمهور ويعكس التنوع الثقافي في المنطقة، مما يجعل هذا الفيلم جزءًا من جهود أكبر لإعادة بناء الهوية الوطنية.

articles.title: رئيس الوزراء وأعضاء حكومة الأمل ينعون الأمير الوالد الشيخ حمد بن خليفة آل ثاني
articles.summary: بقلوب مؤمنة بقضاء الله وقدره ينعى السيد رئيس الوزراء بروفيسور كامل إدريس وأعضاء حكومة الأمل المغفور له بإذن الله صاحب السمو الأمير الوالد الشيخ حمد بن خليفة آل ثاني، الذي إنتقل إلى رحمة الله تعالى بعد مسيرة حافلة بالبذل والعطاء والانجازات في خدمة دولة قطر وشعبها . رئيس الوزراء واعضاء حكومته إذ ينعون الأمير الوالد …

articles.title: Why this energy shock is different
articles.summary: The Gulf ceasefire lasted barely three weeks. After Iranian attacks on three commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz, the United States struck more than 80 targets, revoked Iran’s oil-sanctions waiver, and declared the memorandum of understanding “over.” Yet the market response was telling: Brent crude rose to around $79 per barrel—a meaningful jump, but […]
articles.context: The recent escalation follows the U.S. withdrawal from the Iran nuclear deal in 2018, which has heightened tensions in the Gulf region. The 2020 Abraham Accords, normalizing relations between Israel and several Arab states, further complicated Iran's geopolitical standing. The renewed hostilities threaten to disrupt oil supply routes critical to the Horn of Africa, particularly affecting Ethiopia's energy imports and economic stability amid ongoing internal conflicts and regional diplomatic efforts.

The […]

The recent peace agreement in November 2022 between the federal government and Tigray forces presents a critical opportunity for Ethiopia to stabilize and reshape its economic future.

The city has faced severe shortages of food and medical supplies, exacerbated by the fighting and blockades. The UN's warning of potential mass atrocities highlights the urgent need for international intervention and accountability, particularly following the Juba Peace Agreement's failure to stabilize the region.

articles.title: ما الذي يحدث مع اللاجئين السودانيين والسوريين في مصر؟
articles.summary: وثقت بي بي سي عدة شهادات لمحتجزين ومرحلين من الجنسيتين السودانية والسورية، وسط تقارير حقوقية ترصد ما وصف بـ"حملة أمنية واسعة" تستهدف اللاجئين وطالبي اللجوء والمهاجرين في مصر على مدار شهري ديسمبر/كانون الأول ويناير/كانون الثاني الماضيين.
articles.context: تشهد مصر منذ ديسمبر 2022 حملة أمنية ضد اللاجئين، تزامناً مع تصاعد الأزمات في السودان وسوريا. في السودان، أدت النزاعات المستمرة بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع إلى نزوح جماعي، بينما يعاني السوريون من تداعيات الحرب الأهلية المستمرة منذ 2011. هذه الظروف تجعل اللاجئين عرضة للاحتجاز والترحيل، مما يثير قلق المنظمات الحقوقية حول حقوق الإنسان في مصر.

articles.title: Outgoing Opposition MP Decries Unresolved Conflicts, Unbearable Cost of Living
articles.summary: Protracted conflict in Tigray, Amhara, Oromia, Gambella, and other parts of the country is inflicting a heavy toll on public safety and national stability, Desalegn Chane (PhD), opposition leader and outgoing MP, said today as the Prime Minister appeared before Parliament to answer questions from lawmakers. Desalegn, a senior figure from the National Movement of […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Ethiopia, particularly since the Tigray War began in November 2020, has exacerbated tensions among various ethnic groups, including the Amhara and Oromia regions. The 2022 Pretoria Agreement aimed to cease hostilities in Tigray, yet violence has persisted in other areas, undermining stability. Desalegn Chane's remarks come amid rising inflation and public discontent, highlighting the urgent need for comprehensive political solutions as Ethiopia approaches critical national elections in 2025.

articles.title: AU and Russia Deepen Ties Ahead of Moscow Summit
articles.summary: The African Union (AU) and Russia have agreed to institutionalize annual high-level political consultations, pledging closer cooperation on security, trade, and global governance ahead of the third Russia–Africa Summit this October in Moscow. The commitment follows high-level talks at the AU Commission headquarters between AUC Chairperson Mahmoud Ali Youssouf and Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov. […]
articles.context: The AU-Russia partnership gains significance amid Ethiopia's ongoing Tigray conflict and the broader geopolitical shifts in the Horn of Africa. Following the 2020 peace agreement between the Ethiopian government and Tigray People's Liberation Front, regional stability remains fragile. Additionally, Russia's growing influence in Africa, exemplified by its military and economic engagements, positions it as a key player in counterbalancing Western interests, particularly in light of the upcoming Russia–Africa Summit scheduled for October 2023.

articles.title: Arab League warns of looming humanitarian, security catastrophe in Sudan's El-Obeid
articles.summary: Pan-Arab body urges stronger international action to prevent wider crisis in El-Obeid, accuses RSF of targeting civilian infrastructure
articles.context: El-Obeid, the capital of North Kordofan, has become a focal point in the ongoing conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), which erupted in April 2023. The RSF's targeting of civilian infrastructure has exacerbated the humanitarian crisis, with over 5 million people displaced nationwide. The Arab League's warning comes as Sudan faces a deteriorating security situation, prompting calls for international intervention to avert further catastrophe.

articles.title: “ENDF Ready to Defend Ethiopia”: Prime Minister
articles.summary: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed (PhD) told lawmakers today the Ethiopian National Defense Force (ENDF) is capable of defending Ethiopia’s sovereignty from internal and external threats. “We know our foreign enemies,” said Abiy, also accusing the TPLF of conspiring with external actors. The PM said TPLF is engaged in forced conscription in Tigray, and sending youth […]
articles.context: The statement comes amid heightened tensions following the resumption of hostilities between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) in late 2020, which led to a devastating civil war. The conflict officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022, but sporadic violence and accusations of TPLF collusion with foreign entities persist, complicating Ethiopia's internal stability and regional security dynamics in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: PM Says Ruling Party Prioritized Inclusive Governance Despite Landslide Election Victory
articles.summary: The incumbent Prosperity Party intentionally did not field candidates in 15 percent of Ethiopia’s electoral constituencies for last month’s elections in a bid to encourage wider opposition participation in governance, according to Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed (PhD). He told lawmakers the ruling party opted earlier this year to carve out space for opposition parties. “A […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant political turmoil since the outbreak of the Tigray conflict in November 2020, which has exacerbated ethnic tensions and led to widespread violence. The Prosperity Party, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, has been under scrutiny for its governance approach amid calls for more inclusive politics. The decision to leave 15 percent of electoral constituencies unfilled in the recent elections, held in June 2021, reflects an attempt to address these challenges and promote opposition engagement.

articles.title: Sudanese army says it shot down FH-95 drone in North Kordofan
articles.summary: Military said it intercepted Chinese-made FH-95 drone in North Kordofan, marking 3rd aircraft of same type downed in past 2 weeks
articles.context: The downing of the FH-95 drone comes amid escalating tensions in Sudan, where the military is engaged in a protracted conflict against the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) since April 2023. This conflict has drawn international attention, with various factions vying for control following the ousting of former President Omar al-Bashir in 2019. The interception of foreign drones highlights the increasing militarization of the conflict and the involvement of external actors in Sudan's ongoing instability.

articles.title: Drone attacks kill 15 civilians in North Kordofan, Sudanese rights group says
articles.summary: Emergency Lawyers says 2 drone strikes hit civilian vehicles, killing 15 people, including 5 women
articles.context: The recent drone strikes in North Kordofan occur amid escalating violence in Sudan, particularly following the outbreak of conflict in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. This ongoing power struggle has exacerbated humanitarian crises, with civilian casualties rising sharply. The Emergency Lawyers group, which reported the strikes, has been documenting human rights violations amid the chaos, highlighting the urgent need for international attention and intervention.

articles.title: "هل خَفُت صوت بريطانيا إزاء ما يحدث في السودان بسبب أموال الإمارات وسوق السلاح؟"- في الفاينانشال تايمز
articles.summary: في عرض الصحف، الثلاثاء، لماذا ينبغي لبريطانيا أن تتحمل مسؤولية إنهاء الحرب في السودان، وكيف حولت الولايات المتحدة خامنئي من "وحش إلى شهيد" في ضوء الجنازة الشعبية المليونية الغاضبة للمرشد الأعلى الإيراني الراحل، وأخيراً تدخل ترامب في كأس العالم أدى إلى "تشويه سمعة اللعبة".
articles.context: تشهد السودان منذ أبريل 2023 صراعًا مسلحًا بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية. تتزايد المخاوف من تأثير الإمارات على الصراع، حيث يُعتقد أنها تدعم بعض الأطراف عبر سوق السلاح. في هذا السياق، تُعتبر بريطانيا، التي كانت لها علاقات تاريخية مع السودان، مسؤولة عن الضغط لإنهاء النزاع وتحقيق الاستقرار في المنطقة.

articles.title: Forex Inflow Tops USD 38bln, Says PM

articles.context: Ethiopia has been navigating a complex political landscape following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The government, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, has focused on economic reforms to stabilize the economy and attract foreign investment amidst ongoing challenges, including inflation and regional tensions. The recent influx of foreign exchange is seen as a crucial indicator of recovery and confidence in the administration's policies.

articles.title: The Untold Conflict at UHRC! Why Some Commissioners Have Turned Against Chairperson Mariam Wangadya

Mariam Wangadya's leadership is now challenged at a time when the commission's role is critical in advocating for justice and accountability in a repressive environment.

This incident underscores the urgent need for reform in the mining industry amid a humanitarian crisis.

articles.title: At least 330 children killed, injured in Sudan in 1st half of 2026: UNICEF
articles.summary: ‘For many children, there is no safe place left,’ says UNICEF representative for Sudan
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, has led to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. The recent surge in casualties among children highlights the urgent need for international intervention and support.

articles.title: RSF-aligned alliance calls Sudan’s El-Obeid city 'legitimate military target' despite international warnings
articles.summary: UN officials warn of humanitarian catastrophe in El-Obeid over ongoing RSF attacks
articles.context: El-Obeid, the capital of North Kordofan, has become a focal point in the ongoing conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), which escalated following the outbreak of violence in April 2023. articles.title: UN migration agency warns RSF attacks could turn Sudan’s El Obeid into ‘another El Fasher’
articles.summary: ‘If we do not act now, we will see another El Fasher,’ International Organization for Migration says
articles.context: El Obeid, the capital of North Kordofan, is currently facing escalating violence from the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), a paramilitary group involved in Sudan's ongoing conflict that erupted in April 2023. This violence echoes the tragic events in El Fasher, the capital of North Darfur, where similar RSF actions led to widespread displacement and humanitarian crises. The International Organization for Migration's warning highlights the urgent need for intervention to prevent further destabilization in Sudan.

articles.title: Cholera, measles, food shortages put 200,000 at risk in Sudan's North Kordofan: Medics
articles.summary: Sudan Doctors Network says more than 100 cases of measles recorded among children in Bara town
articles.context: North Kordofan has been significantly affected by the ongoing conflict in Sudan, particularly following the outbreak of violence in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. The region's health infrastructure has deteriorated, exacerbating vulnerabilities to diseases like cholera and measles. The Sudan Doctors Network's report highlights the urgent need for humanitarian assistance amid a backdrop of political instability and food insecurity, with over 200,000 people at risk of severe health crises.

articles.title: Cholera outbreak kills 30, infects 800 in West Kordofan: Sudanese group
articles.summary: Local relief group reports cholera outbreak across more than 20 villages in state
articles.context: The cholera outbreak in West Kordofan comes amid ongoing instability following the Sudanese military coup in October 2021, which exacerbated humanitarian crises across the country. The region has been plagued by conflict between government forces and various armed groups, complicating access to clean water and healthcare. The Sudanese government’s inability to effectively respond to health crises has heightened the vulnerability of local populations, particularly in rural areas where relief efforts are severely hampered.

articles.title: When preservation becomes distortion
articles.summary: Heritage conservation is often defended in the language of protection. We are told that roofs, shelters, barriers, and “improvements” are necessary to shield fragile monuments from rain, sun, wind, and human contact. Yet the first duty of conservation is not to impose a new idea upon an old site, but to respect the logic of […]
articles.context: In Ethiopia, ongoing tensions related to the Tigray conflict have exacerbated concerns over the preservation of cultural heritage sites, particularly in regions like Tigray, where many historical monuments have suffered damage. The Ethiopian government, alongside international organizations, has initiated restoration efforts, yet these interventions often raise questions about authenticity and respect for local traditions. The 2020-2022 conflict highlighted the need for a balanced approach to conservation that honors historical integrity amidst political upheaval.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s decision to join the New Development Bank: unlocking new avenues for development financing
articles.summary: By Amanuel Tadesse The global development finance architecture is undergoing a profound transformation. As developing countries seek greater access to affordable, sustainable, and diversified sources of financing, new multilateral institutions are emerging to complement traditional international financial mechanisms. Among the most prominent of these institutions is the New Development Bank (NDB), established by the BRICS […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's decision to join the New Development Bank comes amid ongoing economic challenges exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020. The Ethiopian government, under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, is seeking to rebuild and diversify its economy while addressing the humanitarian crisis resulting from the conflict. Joining the NDB, established by BRICS nations in 2014, offers Ethiopia an opportunity to access alternative financing to support its post-conflict recovery and development initiatives.

The Sudanese government, under pressure to address the refugee situation, has initiated organized repatriation efforts, highlighting the urgent need for stability and peace in the region.

The bank reported a gross profit before provisioning and tax of ETB 1.15 billion, reflecting a remarkable 94.2% increase compared to the previous fiscal year. ​This robust financial performance was underpinned by a […]
articles.context: Ahadu Bank's impressive profit surge comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic recovery following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with the Pretoria Agreement in November 2022. As the country stabilizes, the banking sector is witnessing renewed investor confidence and growth opportunities, particularly in light of government reforms aimed at liberalizing the economy. This financial success reflects broader trends in Ethiopia's post-conflict economic landscape, where financial institutions are pivotal for recovery and development.

articles.title: More cows than pupils - what is behind mass school closures in rural Kenya?
articles.summary: More than 2,000 primary schools are facing closure as enrolment numbers plummet.
articles.context: In recent years, Kenya has faced severe droughts exacerbated by climate change, particularly affecting pastoral communities in rural areas. The 2019 National Census revealed a significant decline in school enrolment, with many families prioritizing livestock over education due to economic pressures. The government's 2021 policy aimed at improving access to education has struggled against these challenges, leading to the alarming trend of more cows than pupils in schools, particularly in regions like Turkana and Garissa.

articles.title: Top Kenyan court dismisses appeal by widow of slain Pakistani journalist
articles.summary: Supreme Court rejects Arshad Sharif's widow's bid to compel prosecution, upholds compensation award
articles.context: Arshad Sharif, a prominent Pakistani journalist, was shot dead in Kenya in October 2022 under controversial circumstances, sparking international outcry and allegations of extrajudicial killing. His widow, Javeria Siddique, sought legal recourse to hold Kenyan authorities accountable, reflecting ongoing tensions between media freedom and state security in the region. The Supreme Court's dismissal of her appeal underscores the challenges faced by journalists and their families in seeking justice in politically sensitive cases.

articles.title: DRC Ebola claims 452 lives, says WHO
articles.summary: GENEVA – The Ebola disease outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus (BVD) in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) had claimed 452 lives among 1,460 confirmed cases
articles.context: The ongoing Ebola outbreak in the DRC is particularly significant as it occurs amidst a backdrop of political instability and conflict, notably the resurgence of violence in eastern DRC involving armed groups like the M23. The DRC has faced multiple Ebola outbreaks since 1976, with the most recent one highlighting the challenges of healthcare delivery in conflict zones. The WHO's response is crucial, especially given the country's fragile health infrastructure and the potential for regional spillover effects.

articles.title: Kenya’s forex reserves hit record high of 14 bln USD
articles.summary: NAIROBI – Kenya’s foreign exchange reserves rose by 874 million U.S. dollars this week to reach a record high of about 14 billion dollars, the Central Bank of
articles.context: Kenya's surge in foreign exchange reserves comes amid ongoing economic recovery efforts following the COVID-19 pandemic and rising inflation pressures. The Central Bank of Kenya has implemented measures to stabilize the shilling, which has faced depreciation due to regional instability and global economic challenges. Additionally, the recent signing of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement in 2021 has positioned Kenya to leverage its forex reserves to enhance trade and investment opportunities across the continent.

articles.title: ESX Admits Prime Capital as Trading Member
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Securities Exchange (ESX) has welcomed Prime Capital S.C. as a Trading Member, marking another step in the continued expansion of the nation’s capital market infrastructure. The
articles.context: The admission of Prime Capital S.C. as a Trading Member of the Ethiopian Securities Exchange comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing efforts to modernize its financial sector, particularly following the 2018 reforms initiated by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed. These reforms aim to attract foreign investment and stabilize the economy, which has been challenged by internal conflicts, particularly in the Tigray region, and the need for economic recovery post-COVID-19.

articles.title: Ethiopian Airlines Commences Passenger service to Lyon, France
articles.summary: Ethiopian Airlines has expanded its global reach with a commencement of a new thrice weekly passenger service to Lyon, France, as of Thursday July 2, 2026. The airline
articles.context: Ethiopian Airlines' new service to Lyon comes amid ongoing efforts to strengthen international ties following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which strained Ethiopia's diplomatic relations. The airline, a key player in the African aviation sector, aims to boost tourism and trade as the country seeks economic recovery and stability. This expansion aligns with Ethiopia's commitment to enhancing connectivity and fostering partnerships, particularly in light of the African Continental Free Trade Area agreement implemented in January 2021.

articles.title: Addis Bank Registers 5 million shares with Regulators ahead of Planned Listing
articles.summary: Addis Bank S.C. has officially registered 5 million existing shares with the Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA), joining a set of private financial institutions ready to hit the
articles.context: Addis Bank's registration of 5 million shares comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic reforms, initiated in 2018 under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, aimed at liberalizing the banking sector. This move is particularly significant as it follows the establishment of the Ethiopian Capital Market Authority in 2021, which seeks to enhance transparency and attract foreign investment. The bank's listing reflects a broader trend of private financial institutions entering the market, signaling a shift in Ethiopia's economic landscape.

articles.title: Sudanese army battles to retake strategic city near Ethiopian border, military sources say
articles.summary: Army seeks to regain control of Kurmuk in Blue Nile state after RSF, SPLM-N seized city in March
articles.context: The city of Kurmuk, located in Sudan's Blue Nile state near the Ethiopian border, has been a focal point of conflict since the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) clashed in April 2023. The RSF, alongside the Sudan People's Liberation Movement-North (SPLM-N), captured Kurmuk in March, complicating the already volatile situation following the outbreak of civil war in Sudan in April 2023, which has drawn in regional dynamics involving Ethiopia.

articles.title: Why Digital Innovation Keeps Upgrading PH Bingo Online
articles.summary: PH Bingo online continues gaining traction in the Philippines as more players look for familiar fun that suits modern schedules. Bingo has always delivered a distinct kind of excitement—the suspense of waiting for the next number and checking whether it completes a winning pattern. That same anticipation remains, yet online platforms now refine how players […]
articles.context: As Ethiopia navigates a complex political landscape following the Tigray conflict, the government has been focusing on digital transformation to boost economic recovery and engage citizens. The rise of online gaming platforms, including bingo, reflects a broader trend of digital innovation in the region, particularly as the Ethiopian youth increasingly seek entertainment options that align with their fast-paced lifestyles. This shift is significant as it highlights the intersection of technology and social change in a post-conflict society.

articles.title: Can Girmay recreate past Tour de France glories with new team?
articles.summary: Biniam Girmay, who is now backed by a World Cup winner, is aiming to reclaim the green jersey two years after the Eritrean made history at the Tour de France.
articles.context: Biniam Girmay made history in 2022 as the first Black African to win a stage at the Tour de France, symbolizing a breakthrough for African cyclists. His recent partnership with World Cup-winning team manager Patrick Lefevere comes amid ongoing challenges in Eritrea, including political repression and economic hardship. As Girmay aims for the green jersey again, his success could inspire a new generation of athletes in a region often overshadowed by conflict.

articles.title: Ethiopia Set to Become a Technology Destination for Apple
articles.summary: Apple Inc., the California-based global technology giant, is taking a major step toward expanding its market presence in East Africa. In high-level delegate discussions held in Addis Ababa on July 2, 2026, representatives from the technology company engaged in strategic talks with Ethiopian government officials—a significant development that could sustainably transform the country’s growing digital […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been navigating a complex political landscape following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The government, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, is now focused on economic recovery and digital transformation to attract foreign investment. Apple's interest comes amid Ethiopia's efforts to position itself as a technology hub in East Africa, leveraging its young population and improving infrastructure to foster innovation and economic growth.

articles.title: Residents of Ethiopian town forced to kill hundreds of their own dogs after rabies deaths
articles.summary: Some residents reluctantly hung their dogs to avoid being arrested or fined, locals tell the BBC.
articles.context: In 2021, Ethiopia faced a resurgence of rabies amid ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which strained public health resources. The government's focus on military operations has diverted attention from disease control, leading to increased rabies cases and fatalities. The recent deaths of several residents in the town of Gimbi have prompted local authorities to enforce drastic measures, including the culling of dogs, raising concerns about public health and animal rights in a country grappling with multiple crises.

articles.title: ماذا تعني كرة القدم للشباب في المنطقة؟
articles.summary: كرة القدم أكثر من مجرد لعبة بالنسبة للكثير من الشباب في منطقة الشرق الأوسط وشمال أفريقيا. فكيف يعبّرون عن شغفهم بها؟ وماذا تعني لهم هذه الرياضة على المستويين الشخصي والاجتماعي؟
articles.context: تعتبر كرة القدم في الصومال وسيلة للتعبير عن الهوية والوحدة بين الشباب، خاصة في ظل النزاع المستمر منذ عقود. بعد انهيار الحكومة في عام 1991، شهدت البلاد صراعات مسلحة وتفكك اجتماعي، مما جعل الرياضة، وخاصة كرة القدم، ملاذًا للشباب. في عام 2022، تم تنظيم دوري كرة القدم الوطني لتعزيز السلام والمصالحة، مما يعكس أهمية اللعبة في إعادة بناء المجتمع.

articles.title: IMF urges NBE to be ready for further monetary tightening if inflation pressures re-emerge
articles.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has urged the National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE) to remain prepared to tighten monetary policy further if a second round of inflationary pressures emerges, emphasizing the need to preserve the macroeconomic gains achieved under Ethiopia’s economic reform program. The IMF’s warning comes as inflation, which briefly fell to single digits […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant economic challenges following the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020 and led to widespread disruption. The government’s economic reform program, initiated in 2018, aimed to stabilize the economy and attract foreign investment. However, persistent inflation, exacerbated by global supply chain issues and local conflicts, has prompted the IMF to caution the NBE about potential monetary tightening to safeguard the fragile economic recovery.

articles.title: Council Advances Bill Approving Ethiopia’s Entry BRICS New Development Bank
articles.summary: The Council of ministers advanced a bill ratifying the treaty for Ethiopia to join the New Development Bank (NDB), a multilateral financial institution established by the founding members
articles.context: Ethiopia's potential membership in the New Development Bank comes amid ongoing economic challenges and political tensions following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. As Ethiopia seeks to stabilize its economy and attract foreign investment, joining the NDB could enhance its access to funding for infrastructure projects. This move also reflects Ethiopia's broader strategy to strengthen ties with emerging economies, particularly in light of shifting geopolitical dynamics in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: منظمة العفو تدعو إلى إرسال قوات للحماية في السودان بعد الكشف عن "تطهير عرقي" مع مخاوف من تكراره في الأُبيّض
articles.summary: اتهامات جديدة لقوات الدعم السريع بارتكاب جرائم ضد الإنسانية وتطهير عرقي في الفاشر، وتحذيرات أممية من تكرار الفظائع في مدينة الأبيض، وتصاعد الأزمة الإنسانية مع تطور القتال وتفشي الكوليرا، فضلاً عن هجوم على قاعدة أممية حدودية.
articles.context: تأتي هذه الدعوة من منظمة العفو في وقت حساس، حيث تصاعدت حدة النزاع بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع منذ اندلاع القتال في أبريل 2023. الفاشر، عاصمة ولاية شمال دارفور، شهدت انتهاكات جسيمة، بينما تتزايد المخاوف من تكرار هذه الفظائع في مدينة الأبيض، مما يعكس عدم الاستقرار المستمر في البلاد بعد سنوات من الصراع والنزاعات العرقية.

articles.title: Sudan's RSF committed crimes against humanity in el-Fasher, Amnesty says
articles.summary: The Amnesty report adds to evidence of atrocities in el-Fasher, which the UN says bore the hallmarks of genocide.
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, particularly since the outbreak of violence in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), has exacerbated humanitarian crises and human rights violations. El-Fasher, the capital of North Darfur, has witnessed severe atrocities, prompting the UN to label the situation as potentially genocidal. The RSF, originally formed from Janjaweed militias, has been implicated in numerous war crimes, raising urgent calls for accountability and justice.

articles.title: Ethiopian Cargo Lands 2026 AFLAS Award for Best African Air Cargo Carrier
articles.summary: Ethiopian Cargo and Logistics Services, Africa’s largest air cargo network, has won the 2026 Asia Freight, Logistics and Supply Chain (AFLAS) Award for Best Air Cargo Carrier –
articles.context: Ethiopian Cargo's recognition comes amid the country's ongoing recovery from the Tigray conflict, which lasted from November 2020 to November 2022, severely impacting its economy and logistics operations. As Ethiopia seeks to re-establish its position as a regional trade hub, this award highlights the resilience and growth of its logistics sector, particularly under the leadership of Ethiopian Airlines Group CEO Mesfin Tasew, who has focused on expanding air cargo capabilities in a challenging environment.

articles.title: Strengthening Families: Lessons from Decades of Alternative Care in Ethiopia
articles.summary: By Martha Alemayehu* Ethiopia is home to one of the youngest and fastest-growing child populations in the world. With nearly 40 percent of the population being children under
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant challenges in child welfare due to ongoing political instability and conflict, particularly following the Tigray War that began in November 2020. The conflict has led to widespread displacement and disruption of social services, exacerbating the need for alternative care solutions for vulnerable children. The Ethiopian government's commitment to reforming child protection policies, highlighted in the 2019 National Child Policy, underscores the urgency of addressing these issues amid a rapidly growing child population.

articles.title: حتى الشباب والأصحّاء يُنصحون بتجنب الجري والإفراط في شرب الكحول خلال موجات الحر
articles.summary: وسط موجة حار تشهدها المملكة المتحدة، يحذر الخبراء من تزايد حالات توقف القلب خلال فترات الطقس شديد الحرارة، مشيرين إلى أن تأثير الطقس الحار لن يؤثر فقط على كبار السن وذوي البنية الضعيفة فحسب، بل من الممكن أن يطال الأصحاء وأصحاب اللياقة البدنية.
articles.context: تأتي هذه التحذيرات في وقت تعاني فيه منطقة القرن الأفريقي، بما في ذلك الصومال، من آثار تغير المناخ، مما يزيد من حدة موجات الحر. في عام 2022، شهدت الصومال أسوأ موجة جفاف منذ عقود، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأزمات الصحية. مع تزايد الضغوط على النظام الصحي، يصبح من الضروري توعية الناس حول المخاطر الصحية المرتبطة بالحرارة، خاصة في ظل عدم الاستقرار السياسي والاقتصادي الذي تشهده البلاد.

articles.title: Abay Bank S.C. Debuts on ESX Main Market
articles.summary: Abay Bank S.C. has officially been listed on the Ethiopian Securities Exchange (ESX), becoming the fourth commercial bank to make its stock market debut. The Exchange welcomed the
articles.context: Abay Bank S.C.'s listing on the Ethiopian Securities Exchange comes amid ongoing economic reforms initiated by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed since 2018, aimed at liberalizing the economy and attracting foreign investment. This move follows the 2020 establishment of the ESX, which reflects Ethiopia's efforts to stabilize its economy despite challenges posed by the Tigray conflict and inflation. The bank's debut signals a growing confidence in the financial sector's resilience and potential for growth.

articles.title: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
articles.summary: Protesters demand justice for the dozens killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.
articles.context: The protests in Kenya stem from widespread discontent over the government's handling of economic issues and alleged electoral fraud during the 2022 presidential elections, which saw William Ruto assume power amid accusations of irregularities. The 2024 demonstrations, marked by violent clashes with security forces, resulted in the deaths of dozens, prompting ongoing calls for accountability from human rights organizations and opposition leaders like Raila Odinga, who continue to challenge the government's legitimacy.

articles.title: Kenyans mark two years since Gen Z protests
articles.summary: Two years on, families of those killed during Kenya’s 2024 anti-government protests returned to Parliament to remember their loved ones and demand justice.
articles.context: In 2024, Kenya experienced widespread anti-government protests sparked by allegations of electoral fraud and rising living costs, leading to violent clashes between demonstrators and security forces. The protests resulted in numerous fatalities, with families of victims continuing to seek accountability amid a political landscape marked by tensions between President William Ruto's administration and opposition figures like Raila Odinga. The ongoing struggle for justice underscores the fragility of Kenya's democratic institutions and the need for reforms.

articles.title: طلاب سودانيون يعودون إلى معهد النور للمكفوفين برغم الدمار
articles.summary: تفاقمت معاناة المكفوفين في السودان منذ اندلاع الحرب بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع، بعدما تعرض المعهد الوحيد الذي يقدم لهم الخدمات التعليمية لدمار واسع.
articles.context: تسارعت وتيرة الصراع في السودان منذ أبريل 2023، عندما اندلعت اشتباكات عنيفة بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى تدهور الأوضاع الإنسانية. معهد النور للمكفوفين، الذي يعد المعهد الوحيد في البلاد المخصص لتعليم المكفوفين، تعرض لدمار كبير، مما زاد من معاناة هذه الفئة المهمشة في ظل غياب الدعم الحكومي والموارد اللازمة لإعادة تأهيله.

articles.title: Central Bank’s FX Auction Sees Average rate Settles at 157 Birr per Dollar
articles.summary: The average rate of Ethiopian Birr (ETB) has slightly strengthened against the US dollar in the foreign exchange trading on Tuesday, the results released by the Central Bank
articles.context: Ethiopia's economy has faced significant challenges due to the ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which began in November 2020, leading to disruptions in trade and inflation. The government’s efforts to stabilize the economy include implementing foreign exchange auctions to manage the Birr's value. Recent reforms and international support, particularly from the International Monetary Fund, have aimed to enhance economic resilience amid these ongoing tensions and a global economic downturn.

articles.title: Explainer: Critical constraints risk further Ebola spread as cases surpass 1,000
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – As the Bundibugyo Ebola virus outbreak continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, with cases already surpassing 1,000 and
articles.context: The ongoing Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda is exacerbated by the region's fragile healthcare systems, which have been strained by years of conflict and political instability. The 2021 agreement between Uganda and the DRC to enhance cross-border health collaboration has faced challenges due to resource limitations and mistrust among communities. As of October 2023, the resurgence of Ebola highlights the urgent need for coordinated regional responses amid ongoing health crises in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
articles.summary: Sixteen pupils, aged between 15 and 18, died when a fire broke out in a dormitory at Utumishi Girls' School last month.
articles.context: The tragic fire at Utumishi Girls' School on September 26, 2023, has reignited discussions about school safety and accountability in Kenya, particularly in the wake of ongoing debates about the management of educational institutions. The incident follows a series of school-related tragedies in the country, prompting the Ministry of Education to review safety regulations. The decision to charge the students with murder raises questions about juvenile justice and the broader implications for school governance in Kenya.

articles.title: ECMA Approves Registration of Bunna Bank’s Securities
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA) has finalised the registration of more than 64 million existing and new shares of Bunna Bank S.C, a public notice the regulatory
articles.context: The approval by the Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA) comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic reforms aimed at liberalizing the financial sector, initiated in 2018 under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed. This move is significant as it follows the establishment of the Ethiopian Securities Exchange in 2021, which seeks to enhance investment opportunities and stabilize the economy amidst ongoing challenges, including the Tigray conflict and inflationary pressures affecting the banking sector.

articles.title: Sexual violence increasingly used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan, UN says
articles.summary: The scale and brutality of the sexual attacks is unprecedented and aimed at terrorising communities, the UN says.
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan escalated after the military coup in October 2021, which ousted the transitional government and reignited violence between rival factions, particularly the Sudan Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. The UN's recent findings highlight a disturbing trend of sexual violence being weaponized, particularly in regions like Darfur, where historical grievances and ethnic tensions have fueled brutal attacks since the Darfur conflict began in 2003.

articles.title: Kenya minister halts construction of US-backed Ebola quarantine centre
articles.summary: Aden Duale defied a High Court order to stop building work on a controversial US-funded facility.
articles.context: The construction of the US-backed Ebola quarantine center in Kenya has sparked controversy amid ongoing tensions between the Kenyan government and local communities over health infrastructure. In 2021, the Kenyan High Court ruled against the facility, citing concerns over land use and community consent. Aden Duale's defiance of this ruling highlights the ongoing struggle between government authorities and judicial decisions, reflecting broader issues of governance and accountability in Kenya's political landscape.

articles.title: South Sudan sets December date for long-delayed first-ever election
articles.summary: Plans could yet be scuppered again over logistical issues and continued tensions and violence.
articles.context: South Sudan's first-ever elections, scheduled for December 2023, follow years of civil war that erupted in 2013, leading to a fragile peace agreement in 2018. The Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS) aimed to unify rival factions, but ongoing violence and disputes over security arrangements threaten the electoral process. Key actors include President Salva Kiir and opposition leader Riek Machar, whose rivalry continues to fuel instability in the region.

articles.title: Ethiopian Welcomes First Twin Otter Classic 300-G Aircraft from De Havilland Canada
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – Ethiopian Airlines has taken a delivery of its First Twin Otter Classic 300-G aircraft from De Havilland Canada today, June 22, 2026. The delivery comes
articles.context: Ethiopian Airlines' acquisition of the Twin Otter Classic 300-G comes amid ongoing efforts to revitalize the aviation sector following the COVID-19 pandemic and internal conflicts, particularly in the Tigray region. The Ethiopian government, under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, has been focusing on economic recovery and infrastructure development since the peace agreement with Tigray forces in November 2022, making this delivery a symbol of renewed stability and growth in the region's aviation industry.

articles.title: شعبة مستوردي الوقود: عجز تمويلي بقيمة 326 مليون دولار يفاقم أزمة النقد الأجنبي في السودان

articles.context: تواجه السودان أزمة اقتصادية خانقة منذ انفصال جنوب السودان في 2011، حيث فقدت البلاد حوالي 75% من إنتاجها النفطي. تفاقمت هذه الأزمة بسبب النزاعات المستمرة في دارفور والنيل الأزرق، بالإضافة إلى تأثير العقوبات الاقتصادية الدولية. في عام 2023، أدت التوترات في مضيق هرمز إلى ارتفاع أسعار الوقود، مما زاد من الضغوط على الحكومة الانتقالية في السودان التي تسعى لإصلاح الاقتصاد المتعثر.

articles.title: لجنة المعلمين السودانيين تعلن بداية تنفيذ الإضراب في ولاية الخرطوم
articles.summary: قالت لجنة المعلمين السودانيين إن تنفيذ الإضراب في ولاية الخرطوم بدأ اليوم وفق الجدول المعلن من لجنة الإضراب، مشيرةً إلى استمراره حتى يوم غدٍ الثلاثاء. وأفادت اللجنة في منشور على “فيسبوك”، أنّ اللجنة العليا للإضراب ستعقد اجتماعها يوم الجمعة المقبل لتقييم الخطوة الحالية ودراسة المستجدات واتخاذ ما تراه مناسباً من قرارات وخطوات قادمة. articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطوة في سياق الأزمات المتزايدة في السودان، حيث يعاني القطاع التعليمي من تداعيات الصراع المستمر منذ الإطاحة بالرئيس عمر البشير في 2019. تزايدت الاحتجاجات من قبل المعلمين في ظل تدهور الأوضاع الاقتصادية وعدم استقرار الحكومة الانتقالية، مما أدى إلى دعوات للإضراب كوسيلة للضغط على السلطات لتحقيق مطالبهم.

articles.title: Former Kenyan justice minister blocked from entering Uganda, lawyers' body says
articles.summary: Martha Karua is a lawyer representing detained Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye who is on trial for treason.
articles.context: Martha Karua, a prominent Kenyan lawyer and former justice minister, has been actively involved in Ugandan politics, particularly in defending opposition figures like Kizza Besigye, who has faced multiple arrests and trials under President Yoweri Museveni's regime. The ongoing political tension in Uganda, marked by Besigye's treason trial that began in 2021, underscores the challenges faced by opposition leaders in the region, highlighting the fragile state of democracy and human rights in East Africa.

articles.title: بشير مرسال يوقف موظفين بجنوب دارفور

articles.context: The article addresses the ongoing water crisis in Sudan, exacerbated by the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD) project, which began construction in 2011. Tensions have escalated between Sudan, Ethiopia, and Egypt over water rights and management of the Nile River, particularly following the dam's first filling in July 2020. Sudan's struggle with water scarcity is compounded by internal conflicts and political instability, raising urgent concerns about resource management and regional cooperation.

articles.title: محمية بورتسودان: الفصام والبارانويا وفقدان المناعة..!
articles.summary: إذا كنت تراقب شخصاً ابتلاه الله بحالة من (الاختبال العقلي) فيمكنك بقليل من الجهد أن تتوقّع تحركاته وماذا يريد ن يفعل..! ولكن هذا من المستحيلات بإزاء سلطة البرهان الانقلابية التي تعيش حالة متأخرة من (الجنكبة) والتوَهان والتخبّط والجنون الأشتر و(الرقاعة المُفرطة)..! هذه السلطة الانقلابية بأتباعها وجنرالاتها ومستوزريها وحركاتها وإعلامها و(قوناتها) تريد أن تجمع في ضربة واحدة بين (الحرب والفساد والحوار والانتخابات)..! ولو كان الحال خلطاً بين (السمك واللبن والتمرهندي) لهان الأمر..! ولكنها علاوة على ذلك أصبحت تتحدث بـ(مائة لسان)..! فهناك تصريحات البرهان وجماعته..وهناك (الدعّامة العائدين)..والمستشارين

articles.title: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict

articles.context: Abiy Ahmed's Prosperity Party secured a decisive victory in the June 2021 elections, despite ongoing ethnic tensions and violence in regions like Tigray, where a brutal civil war has persisted since November 2020. articles.title: Boy, 12, wins hearts after trying to check sick chicken into Ethiopian hospital

articles.context: In Ethiopia, ongoing conflicts, particularly in the Tigray region since November 2020, have strained healthcare resources and access, affecting both humans and animals. Markos's heartfelt attempt to seek medical help for his sick hen highlights the deep emotional bonds between families and their livestock, reflecting broader societal challenges in a country grappling with the impacts of conflict and economic instability.

articles.title: 'I buried my parents one day after the other' - Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely
articles.summary: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.
articles.context: Sudan is grappling with a resurgence of Ebola amidst ongoing political instability following the military coup in October 2021, which has exacerbated the country's healthcare challenges. The World Health Organization declared an Ebola outbreak in Sudan in September 2023, prompting urgent public health measures. Traditional burial practices, which often involve large gatherings, have been curtailed to prevent further spread of the virus, significantly impacting the grieving process for families affected by the outbreak.

articles.title: Inflation Jumps 13.4% in May on Annual Basis
articles.summary: Inflation slowed in May but continues rising at double digit rate, squeezing finances of consumers, pricing data released by the Ethiopian Statistics Services (ESS) shows. Consumer Price Index
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with economic instability exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. Despite the cessation of hostilities, the country faces ongoing challenges, including supply chain disruptions and rising food prices, which have contributed to persistent inflation. The Ethiopian government’s efforts to stabilize the economy are critical as it seeks to rebuild and address the needs of a population still recovering from years of conflict.

articles.title: تقرير أممي يتهم أطراف الحرب في السودان باستخدام الاعتقال والتعذيب لترهيب المدنيين
